title: STAT+: At hospital finance conference, a call to end the friction that’s keeping costs high
summary: Hospital execs agree that affordability is a major issue. What to do about it is less clear.

title: Opinion: Ebola outbreak response requires engaging with religious traditions and leaders
summary: “Failing to engage religious and traditional leaders in meaningful ways was a grave misstep in previous Ebola outbreaks,” researchers write.

title: Opinion: I’ve spent 40 years in research. I have never seen a threat to science like the new grantmaking rule
summary: “American science is too valuable to be turned into a political football,” writes David J. Skorton, president of the Association of American Medical Colleges.

title: How a diabetes conference sparked a controversy
summary: Many in the diabetes research community expressed shock and disbelief that their colleagues were threatened with arrest for passing out paper copies of an editorial.

summary: STAT is tracking goals and promises of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and his Make America Healthy Again movement — successes, incompletes, and fails.

summary: Can any of the new obesity medications in development stand out from the pack? Which company just broke records with its IPO? And will the Food and Drug Administration allow…

title: RFK Jr. claims his calendar is publicly available. We’ve been trying to get it for a year
summary: None of STAT's requests for Kennedy's schedule have been completed, and some haven't even been acknowledged.

title: Drinking during pregnancy rose after 2020, new CDC data suggest
summary: A new CDC report underscores that drinking during pregnancy, while generally thought of as a thing of the past, is still a matter of concern.

summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: HHS watchdog on denials by health insurers, lawmakers target AI denials, and Talkspace's new chatbot offering.

title: Nonprofit buys experimental cancer drug to maintain patient access
summary: In a rare move, nonprofit organization Blood Cancer United is buying the remaining supplies of a discontinued investigational cancer drug.

title: Study highlights influence of socioeconomic status on children’s brain development
summary: A new study found that the leading environmental factor influencing brain structure and function is the socioeconomic status of a child's family.

title: STAT+: Abridge inks deals with Nvidia and Lilly
summary: Abridge announced new deals with Eli Lilly and Nvidia as it aims to gain an edge in a competitive market.

title: Summit pulls $500M share sale a day after announcing it
summary: The developer of a leading PD-1/VEGF cancer drug cited “market conditions” for the cancellation. Its shares have struggled to break out following data milestones.

title: Sensorion, citing Regeneron competition, shifts focus to a different hearing loss therapy
summary: A strategic review determined that the development environment had "notably changed," leading Sensorion to end work on its gene therapy for OTOF-related hearing loss.

title: STAT+: A suspicious denial pattern in Medicare Advantage
summary: Major Medicare Advantage plans almost always reversed their denials on appeal, raising big questions about barriers to care.

title: Novartis RNA drug acquired in $12B Avidity deal notches a trial win
summary: The data showed that del-brax, an “antisense oligonucleotide conjugate” being tested against a muscle-wasting disease, met its primary endpoint in a Phase 1/2 study.

summary: Proving out a "synthetic lethality" approach can help treat cancer will take a lot more work. It also might trigger some creative deal-making.

title: STAT+: Enliven Therapeutics’ leukemia drug shows promise in new study
summary: A targeted drug from Enliven Therapeutics induced molecular responses in nearly half of patients with advanced leukemia, new data show.

title: STAT+: Scientists see promise in NIH proposal to cap number of grants they receive
summary: Without easy access to leaders within a field or top-of-the-line lab equipment, principal investigators outside top universities often struggle to compete for grants from the NIH.

title: Opinion: How long Covid’s scientific stalemate made it politically erasable
summary: Long Covid hasn’t disappeared. It’s just gone underground, writes Steven Phillips.

title: Sanofi stops immune drug trial in latest research setback
summary: The failure adds to a series of mixed or negative readouts and leaves in doubt the future of a therapy Sanofi has viewed as a potential blockbuster.

title: Parabilis sets a record with a $670M biotech IPO
summary: The largest-ever new stock sale for a venture-backed biotech company extends a streak of large IPOs in 2026 that’s now resulted in 12 drug startups securing more than $4.1 billion combined.

title: Stealthy startup Treeline plans a reverse merger
summary: Led by former Loxo Oncology CEO Josh Bilenker, the startup now has a trio of cancer drugs in early-stage testing and more following closely behind.
